rationale_for_baseline_tier: Localising RLQ pain that cannot be examined remotely, where appendicitis is a can't-miss differential, warrants urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) with symptomatic care and a strict peritonism safety-net — escalating to T4 (ED) if surgical features appear. A reassuring history does not substitute for an abdominal examination.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Constant severe or worsening pain, rigidity, marked pain on movement/coughing, fever >38C, or vomiting
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain becomes constant and severe, your tummy goes hard, it hurts to move or cough, you get a fever, or you start vomiting, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Partner at home with a car
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Can be driven for same-day assessment; call 000 if severe or rapidly worsening.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is likely a gut flare, but because I can't examine your tummy, you need to be seen in person today to rule out your appendix. Take paracetamol and clear fluids meanwhile. If the pain becomes constant and severe, your tummy goes rigid, or you get a fever or vomiting, go to ED straight away.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend urgent care/ED tonight if red flags develop or you cannot be seen same-day.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of colicky RLQ pain, likely Crohn's flare but appendicitis not excludable remotely; urgent in-person assessment advised; peritonism safety-net given; CRP/calprotectin and gastroenterology review recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring RLQ pain as a flare with only routine follow-up and no in-person assessment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An appendicitis is mislabelled a flare; it perforates overnight causing peritonitis.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T3 is correct; risk is failing to escalate on red flags.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for stable colicky pain is over-triage; same-day in-person assessment (T3) or ED self-transport (T4) suffices unless severe.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the abdomen or obtain markers/imaging remotely to exclude appendicitis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment with symptomatic care and a strict peritonism safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure as a Crohn's flare remotely with only routine follow-up.
